title: lichen_iface static pointer written once, read without mutex
description:
The static struct net_if *lichen_iface in lichen_l2.c is written in lichen_l2_iface_init() (line 404) and read in lora_rx_callback() (line 352) without synchronization.

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:88, 352, 404

The code has an extensive comment (lines 76-87) explaining why this is safe:
- Pointer is set exactly once in init
- Never cleared
- net_if has device lifetime
- RX callback registration happens AFTER the pointer is set (line 407)

What goes wrong: The safety relies on the ordering guarantee that set_rx_callback() (line 407) happens after lichen_iface assignment (line 404). If a future refactor moves callback registration earlier, the RX callback could read a NULL or stale pointer.

The comment explicitly warns against clearing the pointer, which is good. However, the write-read ordering is not enforced by the compiler - it relies on statement ordering and Zephyr's callback registration semantics.

Options:
1. Accept current design (comment documents the invariant)
2. Use atomic_ptr_set/get for defense in depth
3. Add static_assert or runtime check

Filing as P2 since the invariant is well-documented and current code is correct.
close_reason: Existing comment at lines 89-101 in lichen_l2.c is adequate - documents write-once invariant, thread-safety rationale, and references issue ybal.4
